observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer exercise prudence in the utilization of the available resources ensuring that only highly necessary and legal expenditures are being paid and that monies held in trust be used only for their intended purposes.

We recommended further that Municipal officials and employees observe austerity measures on its operation to eliminate the cash deficit.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances amounting to P9,554,956.91 pursuant to existing regulations, otherwise require the persons accountable to refund the cash advances granted to them.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to refrain from granting additional cash advances to employees and officers with unliquidated cash advances, and to strictly comply with the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer exercise prudence in the utilization of the available resources, ensure that only highly necessary and legal expenditures are being paid, and fund held in trust be used only for their intended purposes.
We recommended further and Management agreed that Municipal officials and employees observe austerity measures on its operation to address the finances of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Inventory Committee to undertake the required physical inventory of PPEs and submit to the Auditor an updated RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.  After which, appropriate adjustments shall be made by the Municipal Accountant on PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed thru the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to closely monitor the timeline of implementation of 20% Development Fund projects for the timely delivery of benefits to the constituents.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed that the unexpended amount of P1,314,157.94 of LDRRMF for CY 2013 to 2018 shall be recorded in the Trust Fund books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.  A copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the said transfer with the updated balance should be submitted to the Auditor as proof of compliance.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary reversion of the outstanding balance of the BGCM in the accounting books to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended and the Municipal officials agreed that the Program Coordinator at the Municipal Health Office furnish the Municipal Accountant with the approved detailed list of allocated vaccines received and utilized by the Municipality, and to recognize in the books the donations in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended and the management agreed that reconciliation of records between the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer be undertaken pursuant to Section 266, GAM, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments in the accounting books to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement.

Furthermore, we recommended and was also agreed that same offices investigate the noted differences and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.
observations.managementAction: Started the physical inventory taking but was not able to complete the report on time.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report..

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances amounting to P8,678,138.06 pursuant to existing regulations, otherwise require the persons accountable to refund the cash advances granted to them.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant also agreed not to grand additional cash advances to employees and officers with unliquidated cash advances, and to strictly comply with the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of the same.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the required RSMI and RPCI for proper recording of Inventories to the proper Expense account/s and appropriate adjustment to the Prior Year’s to fairly present the amount in the financial statements.

Furthermore, it was recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to conduct periodic physical count of supplies and materials, prepare and submit the required reports in accordance with the existing guidelines.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to analyze accounts with abnormal balances, effect the necessary adjustments in the books and submit the corresponding copy/ies of the Journal Vouchers and supporting documents for evaluation.

Subsequently, the conduct of regular monitoring shall be done periodically to promptly effect correcting entries.
observations.managementAction: The amount was validated.
observations.reason: Amount was partially decreased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LGU agreed that the Municipal Mayor thru the BAC Secretariat require duly complied Omnibus Sworn Statements from the suppliers, and strictly require the submission of the OSS for subsequent Emergency Purchases in case the supplier fails to submit complete documentary requirements after the contract has been awarded.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer thoroughly examines projects in the continuing appropriations, especially those that remained unobligated at year-end and make representation with the Local Chief Executive that appropriations no longer needed be recommended for immediate reversion to unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to fund other viable programs and projects of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Request for reversion of some appropriations had been submitted to Sangguiang Bayan.
observations.reason: There are still continuing appropriations that were not obligated as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to monitor the completeness of the DVs from initial receipt until after recording in the journals in order to timely submit all DVs with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

Further, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant also agreed to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and the corresponding supporting documents of the 59 checks issued totaling P5,603,568.20 to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise, Notices of Suspension for these disbursements will be issued.
observations.managementAction: The submission of DVs were reviewed and updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board review and update their existing SWM Plan to include required components and submit the same to the National Commission for approval in order to comply with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The SWM Board was reactivated to update the SWM Plan already prepared.
observations.reason: Lack of funds for the preparation of a landfill as required.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to demand the concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers of their immediate submission of liquidation reports, and subsequently adjust and close the account Due from LGUs for the funds were already expended by the recipient barangays.

It is also recommended that management constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to submit liquidation reports as soon as the purpose of the fund transfer have been served, otherwise, no additional fund transfers shall be released.
observations.managementAction: Required all barangays to settle unliquidated fund transfer as of December 31, 2019 and some barangays had settled their accounts.
observations.reason: There are still fund transfers made in prior years that were not settled as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant process and release funds to legitimate NGOs/POs with complete supporting documents, and regularly monitor project implementation as well as liquidation of funds as stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already issued a demand letter to Cogon Farmers Association.
observations.reason: Still not settled as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Office, the Municipal Engineers’ Office and other offices that are involved in the implementation of 20% Development Fund projects, immediately address the causes of delays and fast track its implementation. Regular monitoring of project implementation should be assigned to specific office to achieve desirable outcomes from the delivery of socio-economic development and environmental services of the constituents.

We recommended further that management should be more aggressive in the implementation of projects to attain higher performance efficiency level.
observations.managementAction: Some Projects were implemented in the succeeding years.
observations.reason:
There were still appropriations for projects in CY 2019 that remained unutilized as of December 31, 2021.
Reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant process payments on infrastructure projects only if supported with the required documents in accordance with existing regulations, and submit the lacking supporting documents on the identified transactions to prevent the transactions from being suspended in audit.

We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure the completeness of bidding documents necessary for the conduct of bid evaluation until the projects are awarded to Contractor.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately cause the closure of the dormant accounts and cause the transfer of the balance totaling P268, 673.08 to the Municipality’s active count.
observations.managementAction: Worked on the closing of some dormant accounts.
observations.reason: As of December 31 2020, there were two accounts that remained dormant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality fast track the immediate transfer of the titles of the lands in the name of the Municipality of Candoni to establish rightful ownership.
observations.managementAction: Some have titles already, but were not turned-over to the proper custodian. Will update on the titling of all parcels of Land owned by the Municipality.
observations.reason: To date, no follow up action had been communicated to the Office. Notes to FS as of December 31, 2021 still disclosed real properties without TCTs.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Mayor shall require the disposal committee to appraise the property subject of disposal, then file a request for disposal of unserviceable properties supported with necessary documents with the Auditor for technical evaluation and appraisal.

Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer shall also coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for proper classification of the unserviceable items to Other Asset Account.
observations.managementAction: On-going physical count of PPE and Inventory which is expected to be completed in CY 2021.
observations.reason: No request had been submitted for disposal of unserviceable properties in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that required documentary requirements are duly attached to the disbursement vouchers before approving any release of fund transfers to various barangays pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with the guidelines in the required documents, in fact for CY 2019, no fund transfers were made to barangays unless the previous fund transfers were fully liquidated.
observations.reason: Not all fund transfers made before CY 2018 were settled as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipality of Candoni should submit the required documentary requirements pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, otherwise the transactions will be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, the agency issued the Office Memorandum prescribing documentary requirements for common local government transactions and checklist requirements to secure the completeness of documents to be attached.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that reconciliation of records between the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer be undertaken pursuant to Section 266, GAM, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments in the accounting books to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement.
Furthermore, we recommended and was also agreed that same offices investigate the noted differences and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer exercise prudence in the utilization of the available resources by ensuring that only highly necessary and legal expenditures are being paid and that monies held in trust be used only for their intended purposes.
We recommended further that Municipal officials and employees observe austerity measures on its operation to eliminate the cash deficit.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances amounting to P8,678,138.06 pursuant to existing regulations, otherwise require the persons accountable to refund the cash advances granted to them.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant also agreed not to grand additional cash advances to employees and officers with unliquidated cash advances, and to strictly comply with the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of the same.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the required RSMI and RPCI for proper recording of Inventories to the proper Expense account/s and appropriate adjustment to the Prior Year’s to fairly present the amount in the financial statements.
Furthermore, it was recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to conduct periodic physical count of supplies and materials, prepare and submit the required reports in accordance with the existing guidelines.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to analyze accounts with abnormal balances, effect the necessary adjustments in the books and submit the corresponding copy/ies of the Journal Vouchers and supporting documents for evaluation.  Subsequently, the conduct of regular monitoring shall be done periodically to promptly effect correcting entries.

We recommended and the LGU agreed that the Municipal Mayor thru the BAC Secretariat require duly complied Omnibus Sworn Statements from the suppliers, and strictly require the submission of the OSS for subsequent Emergency Purchases in case the supplier fails to submit complete documentary requirements after the contract has been awarded.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer thoroughly examines projects in the continuing appropriations, especially those that remained unobligated at year-end and make representation with the Local Chief Executive that appropriations no longer needed be recommended for immediate reversion to unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to fund other viable programs and projects of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to monitor the completeness of the DVs from initial receipt until after recording in the journals in order to timely submit all DVs with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
Further, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant also agreed to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and the corresponding supporting documents of the 59 checks issued totaling P5,603,568.20 to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise, Notices of Suspension for these disbursements will be issued.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board review and update their existing SWM Plan to include required components and submit the same to the National Commission for approval in order to comply with existing regulations.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately cause the closure of the dormant accounts and cause the transfer of the balance totaling P268,673.08 to the Municipality’s active count.
observations.reason: Per verification, no action taken by the Agency. Those accounts are still not closed and still carried in the book of the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Municipal Mayor shall require the disposal committee to appraise the property subject of disposal, then file a request for disposal of unserviceable properties supported with necessary documents with the Auditor for technical evaluation and appraisal. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer shall also coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for proper classification of the unserviceable items to Other Asset Account.
observations.reason: The Municipal Property Custodian promised to file a request for those identified unserviceable motor vehicles, however, no request was received by our Office to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of its Inventories and PPEs, prepare and submit reports thereon pursuant to Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. We recommended also that the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian shall reconcile their records. Hereafter, physical count of Inventories and PPEs and reconciliation of Accounting and Property Custodian records shall be regularly done to ensure the accuracy of the reported balance of inventories and PPE accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in current audit findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipality of Candoni should submit the required documentary requirements pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, otherwise the transactions will be suspended in audit.
observations.reason: For CY 2019, there are still payments with incomplete documents. In fact, this finding was reiterated in current audit findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality of Candoni formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRM Plan in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the Local Development Councils (LDCs) taking into consideration the local needs under four aspects: prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery.
observations.reason: For CY 2019, the LDRRM Plan is still not in accordance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. 10121.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant further analyze and evaluate unpaid obligations that remained outstanding for more than two years totaling P288,561.28 and effect its reversion to the unappropriated surplus, pursuant to Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.reason: No reversion was made on the long outstanding balance of “Accounts Payable” account. Reiterated in current audit findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of supplies and materials and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to concerned offices not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit to the Auditor an updated inventory report on the physical count of the properties not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the propriety and validity of all outstanding payables. For accounts with no valid claimants or are not covered by any perfected contract, revert it to Surplus Account to fairly present the Accounts Payable account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant with a certified list of taxpayers, as well as its total amount due and collectible for the year 2019, and years after to recognize and record the same correctly in the books of accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that management strictly observe the existing CSC rules and regulations in the processing of monetization of leave credits of its officials and employees. Adjust/correct leave cards of employees with erroneous entries or recording, and require concerned personnel who were already paid of monetization to submit the required lacking documents to the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant process only claims that have complete supporting documents required for every type of transactions and ensure that all personnel are informed of these requirements by preparing and disseminating a checklist of the required documents for every type of transactions. For deficiency noted while processing a transaction, a deficiency slip shall be attached to the voucher and returned to the appropriate department for compliance.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to demand the concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers of their immediate submission of liquidation reports, and subsequently adjust and close the account Due from LGUs for the funds were already expended by the recipient barangays.
It is also recommended that management constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to submit liquidation reports as soon as the purpose of the fund transfer have been served, otherwise, no additional fund transfers shall be released.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant process and release funds to legitimate NGOs/POs with complete supporting documents, and regularly monitor project implementation as well as liquidation of funds as stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant deposit immediately the unutilized amount to the Bureau of Treasury and submit the Report of Disbursement or Liquidation Report to the source agencies. Strictly observe the provisions of the MOA, especially on immediate submission of liquidation reports and the return of unutilized balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Office, the Municipal Engineers’ Office and other offices that are involved in the implementation of 20% Development Fund projects, immediately address the causes of delays and fast track its implementation. Regular monitoring of project implementation should be assigned to specific office to achieve desirable outcomes from the delivery of socio-economic development and environmental services of the constituents.
We recommended further that management should be more aggressive in the implementation of projects to attain higher performance efficiency level.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant process payments on infrastructure projects only if supported with the required documents in accordance with existing regulations, and submit the lacking supporting documents on the identified transactions to prevent the transactions from being suspended in audit.
We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure the completeness of bidding documents necessary for the conduct of bid evaluation until the projects are awarded to Contractor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal LDRRMO and/or the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare a monthly Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF and submit the same to the Audit Team within the prescribed period for assessment and monitoring purposes.
We recommended that the Municipality through its LDRRM Council to review its LDRRM Plan, consolidate all unexpended funds from previous years and formulate viable projects and activities to be implemented in the ensuing year.  Relatively, the LDDRM Officer ensures the timely utilization of the fund in order to improve the LGU's ability to prepare for and respond to threats of calamities and disasters.

We recommended that Management through the GAD Focal Person submit the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report within five (5) days from end of January of the preceding year together with the required supporting documents. Strict compliance with the existing regulations must be observed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all accountable officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their unliquidated cash advances specifically those granted prior to December 31, 2011 considering that a final demand was already issued pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.reason: Cash advances by non-LGU personnel are not allowed anymore. However, for cash advances by LGU personnel are still unliquidated. Demand letters for those with outstanding cash advances will be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality fast track the immediate transfer of the titles of the lands in the name of the Municipality of Candoni to establish rightful ownership.
observations.reason: Some have titles already, but were not turn-over to the proper custodian. They will update their records as to the land titles of the Land account owned by the Municipality and will process those that without titles. To date, no follow up action was communicated to the Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that required documentary requirements are duly attached to the disbursement vouchers before approving any release of fund transfers to various barangays pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant complied with the guidelines in the required documents, in fact for CY 2019, no fund transfers were made to barangays unless the previous fund transfers were fully liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: BUB Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of all BUB projects so that the optimum goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction could be fully attained; and Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately refund/remit the excess BUB project funds to the source national government agencies to avoid possible loss or misuse.
observations.managementAction: Majority of the BuB projects were already implemented completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that only programs/projects/activities embodied in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 be funded from the 20% Development Fund in order to achieve its objectives.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality complied with the findings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned personnel submit to the COA Auditor the copies of the perfected contracts on infrastructure projects within five (5) days from perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Some the copies of contracts were submitted to our office.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 for those granted before December 31, 2011. The Municipal Accountant require the accountable officers to strictly comply with the guidelines provided in Items 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 in the liquidation of their cash advances. The Municipal Accountant must monitor the liquidation and demand settlement if no liquidation was submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances by non-LGU personnel are not allowed anymore. However, for cash advances by LGU personnel are still unliquidated. Demand letters for those with outstanding cash advances will be made.
observations.reason: Demand letters for those with outstanding cash advances will be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical count of its Inventories and PPEs, prepare and submit reports thereon within the prescribed deadline pursuant to Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Training Handbook on Property & Supply Management System, to have an assurance that the amounts of inventories and PPEs are fairly presented in the financial statements. We recommended also that the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian shall reconcile their records. Hereafter, physical count of Inventories and PPEs and reconciliation of Accounting and Property Custodian records shall be regularly done to ensure the accuracy of the reported balance of inventories and PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly cause the remittance in full the amount withheld to the BIR within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding accrued. This practice shall be constantly enforced.
observations.reason: There are still unremitted taxes as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality adhere strictly to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 by submitting the quarterly report on Programs/Projects/Activities to COA within the legal time frame.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the quarterly report on PPAs every quarter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should Allocate fund for the Local Councils for the Protection of Children, pursuant to Section 15 of RA 9344.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, the Municipality has already appropriated P1,005,601.00 for 1% Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that   
the Municipal         
Accountant further    
analyze and evaluate  
unpaid obligations    
that remained         
outstanding for more  
than two years        
totaling P288,561.28  
and effect its        
reversion to the      
unappropriated        
surplus, pursuant to  
Section 98 of         
Presidential Decree   
No. 1445.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately cause the closure of the dormant accounts and cause the transfer of the balance totaling P268, 673.08 to the Municipality’s active count.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all accountable officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their unliquidated cash advances specifically those granted prior to December 31, 2011 considering that a final demand was already issued pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
The Municipal Accountant ensure that all advances to Officers and Employees and Special Disbursing Officer are liquidated within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Municipality fast track the immediate transfer of the titles of the lands in the name of the Municipality of Candoni to establish rightful ownership.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor shall require the disposal committee to appraise the property subject of disposal, then file a request for disposal of unserviceable properties supported with necessary documents with the Auditor for technical evaluation and appraisal.
Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer shall also coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for proper classification of the unserviceable items to Other Asset Account.

We recommended that the Municipality direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of its Inventories and PPEs, prepare and submit reports thereon pursuant to Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to have an assurance that the amounts of inventories and PPEs in the financial statements are fairly presented.
We recommended also that the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian shall reconcile their records.
Hereafter, physical count of Inventories and PPEs and reconciliation of Accounting and Property Custodian records shall be regularly done to ensure the accuracy of the reported balance of inventories and PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that required documentary requirements are duly attached to the disbursement vouchers before approving any release of fund transfers to various barangays pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
BUB Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of all BUB projects so that the optimum goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction could be fully attained; and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately refund/remit the excess BUB project funds to the source national government agencies to avoid possible loss or misuse.

We recommend that Municipality of Candoni should submit the required documentary requirements pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, otherwise the transactions will be suspended in audit.
We recommended that only programs/projects/activities embodied in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 be funded from the 20% Development Fund in order to achieve its objectives.

We recommended that the Municipality of Candoni formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated LDRRM Plan in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction in close coordination with the Local Development Councils (LDCs) taking into consideration the local needs under four aspects: prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that the concerned personnel submit to the COA Auditor the copies of the perfected contracts on infrastructure projects within five (5) days from perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 for those granted before December 31, 2011.
The Municipal Accountant require the accountable officers to strictly comply with the guidelines provided in Items 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 in the liquidation of their cash advances.
The Municipal Accountant must monitor the liquidation and demand settlement if no liquidation was submitted within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical count of its Inventories and PPEs, prepare and submit reports thereon within the prescribed deadline pursuant to Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Training Handbook on Property & Supply Management System, to have an assurance that the amounts of inventories and PPEs are fairly presented in the financial statements.
Hereafter, physical count of Inventories and PPEs and reconciliation of Accounting and General Services Office records shall be regularly done to ensure the accuracy of the reported balance of inventories and PPE accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management review the unutilized balances of the LDRRMF as of December 31, 2017, to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to disaster and climate change impacts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that programs/projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund  should be optimally utilized considering that these are priority projects to provide for  the needs of the constituents’ socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the LGU in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly cause the remittance in full the amount withheld to the BIR within 10 days following the end of the month the withholding was made or the withholding accrued. This practice shall be constantly enforced.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality adhere strictly to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 by submitting the quarterly report on Programs/Projects/Activities to COA within the legal time frame.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant further analyze and evaluate unpaid obligations that remained outstanding for more than two years totaling P288,561.28 and effect its reversion to the unappropriated surplus, pursuant to  Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should Allocate fund for the Local Councils for the Protection of Children, pursuant to Section 15 of RA 9344.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality ensure that the GAD funds be fully utilized and maximized for GAD programs and activities only.
We recommended that the accountable officers liquidate their cash advances pursuant to Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management remit promptly the taxes withheld from the employees to the BIR and monthly premiums, contributions and loan repayments to the GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth in the total amount of P1,078,741.26, as prescribed under Section 6 of Republic Act No. 8291 dated May 30, 1997 and BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17 1998 and Section 6 (a) of PD 1146.

We recommended that the Municipality submit the CY 2016 GAD fund accomplishment report, providing documentation on Gender and Development Programs/Projects, pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-01 dated April 5, 2004 issued by the DBM, NEDA, and Philippine Commission on Women.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality adhere strictly with the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 for the establishment of a DRRM Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that Petty Cash Fund be fully liquidated at year end and all expenses for the year be recorded in the year of occurrence pursuant to Section 48 of NGAS Manual for Local Government Units.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality conduct the Physical Inventory of its properties to comply with the provisions of Section 490 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I and Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.reason: Finding reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit disbursement vouchers, official receipts and supporting documents due for submission to the Audit Team. Henceforth, he must strictly adhere to the deadline of submission of aforementioned documents pursuant to Section 7.2.1 a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Sections 100 and 107 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the  accountable  officers  liquidate  their  cash
advances pursuant to Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the  management  remit  promptly  the  taxes
withheld  from  the  employees  to  the  BIR  and  monthly   premiums,
contributions and loan repayments to the GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth
in the total amount of P1,078,741.26, as prescribed under Section 6 of
Republic Act No. 8291 dated May 30, 1997 and  BIR  Revenue  Regulation
No. 2-98 dated April 17 1998 and Section 6 (a) of PD 1146.

We recommended that the Municipality submit the CY 2016 GAD fund
accomplishment  report,  providing   documentation   on   Gender   and
Development Programs/Projects, pursuant to Joint Circular No.  2004-01
dated April 5, 2004 issued by the DBM, NEDA, and Philippine Commission
on Women.

We recommended that the Municipality adhere  strictly  with  the
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1  dated April
4, 2014 for the establishment of a DRRM Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure  that  Petty
Cash Fund be fully liquidated at year end and  all  expenses  for  the
year be recorded in the year of occurrence pursuant to Section  48  of
NGAS Manual for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the Municipality conduct the Physical
Inventory of its properties to comply with the provisions of Section
490 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I and
Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for
Local Government Units, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately  submit
disbursement vouchers, official receipts and supporting  documents  due  for
submission to the Audit Team.

             Henceforth,  he  must  strictly  adhere  to  the  deadline   of
      submission of aforementioned documents pursuant to Section 7.2.1 a) of
      COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009  and  Sections  100
      and 107 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

management strictly     |     |           |              |               
comply with the         |     |           |              |               
provision of Section 119|     |           |              |               
of PD 1445. Refrain from|     |           |              |               
using current           |     |           |              |               
appropriation/allotment |     |           |              |               
for payment of prior    |     |           |              |               
year’s                  |     |           |              |               
obligations/transactions|     |           |              |               
.                       |     |           |              |               
                        |     |           |              |               
Inform all cost center  |     |           |              |               
heads in advance to     |     |           |              |               
submit supporting       |     |           |              |               
documents to the budget |     |           |              |               
office for obligation of|     |           |              |               
the expenses already    |     |           |              |               
incurred during the year|     |           |              |               
to be charged to the    |     |           |              |               
current year            |     |           |              |               
appropriation.          |     |           |              |               
                        |     |           |              |               
Direct the accounting   |     |           |              |               
unit to take up in the  |     |           |              |               
books of accounts all   |     |           |              |               
valid and legal         |     |           |              |               
expenditures at the end |     |           |              |               
of the calendar year and|     |           |              |               
shall be properly       |     |           |              |               
obligated as accounts   |     |           |              |               
payable in accordance   |     |           |              |               
with laws and           |     |           |              |               
regulations to preclude |     |           |              |               
distortion of the       |     |           |              |               
current annual budget   |     |           |              |               
and possible            |     |           |              |               
misstatement of income. |     |           |              |               
                        |     |           |              |               
Effect the necessary    |     |           |              |               
adjusting entries to    |     |           |              |               
rectify the error, as   |     |           |              |               
follows:                |     |           |              |               
                        |     |           |              |

Pxxx                    |     |           |              |               
Operating Expenses      |     |           |              |               
Pxxx                    |     |           |              |

Vicente P. Petierre,    |     |           |              |

P50,000.00 to the       |     |           |              |

Negros Occidental, since|     |           |              |               
his acceptance of the   |     |           |              |               
fees for legal services |     |           |              |               
is contrary to the above|     |           |              |               
cited provisions.       |     |           |              |

Municipal Accountant    |     |           |              |

the attached partial    |     |           |              |

                        |     |           |              |               
We further recommend    |     |           |              |               
that management exert   |     |           |              |

In case of              |     |           |              |

Municipal Accountant    |     |           |              |               
shall cause the         |     |           |              |
